instruction: Walk forward, take a left around the corner and walk all the way to the end of the hallway, and stop at the door before the bedroom.

**Summary of the route I followed:**
1. **"Walk forward"** — moved forward from the entry/great-room area.
2. **"Take a left around the corner"** — turned left into the corridor with the wood door.
3. **"Walk all the way to the end of the hallway"** — followed the corridor to its end where a new wing opened up (a dining room behind, a sitting nook to the left).
4. **"Stop at the door before the bedroom"** — stopped at the threshold/doorway just before the bedroom (bed with pillows, bench, nightstand lamp, and arched niche clearly visible ahead), without entering it.

The middle of the run was inefficient — the villa has a central hub of archways that I orbited a couple of times before re-anchoring with look_around and confirming I'd returned to the start, then executing the route cleanly. Final stop was made with ~293 movement steps still in budget.
